title: Research GPT and Claude subscription constraints for IDE autocomplete
scope: Determine what is and is not possible using consumer or team subscriptions for ChatGPT/OpenAI and Claude/Anthropic as of 2026-05-28, especially whether those subscriptions include API access usable by VS Code or Obsidian autocomplete plugins. Distinguish official subscription access from separate paid API billing and unofficial browser/session automation.
goals: Use current official sources for ChatGPT/OpenAI and Claude/Anthropic subscription/API claims.

For each subscription tier or access path, record subscription_tier, includes_api_access, direct_extension_use_supported, requires_separate_api_billing, unofficial_session_automation_risk, source_urls, last_checked, and confidence.
Identify whether any official IDE or extension supports using a ChatGPT or Claude subscription directly for inline tab autocomplete.
Summarize legal/ToS, reliability, latency, and security implications of browser-cookie/session scraping if encountered.
List verified source URLs and quote only short snippets if needed.
non_goals: Do not comprehensively evaluate every VS Code extension.
Do not write the final report.
safety: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or prompt-buffer.md.
Do not edit files tagged human-written.
Prefer official sources for claims about subscriptions and API access.
success_criteria: Every major claim has a source URL and confidence level.
Explicitly answer whether GPT/Claude subscriptions can be used directly for tab-style autocomplete.
